item.title: Moses cleared as Blues aim to wrap up Origin series tonight
item.description: Moses cleared as Blues aim to wrap up Origin series tonight

item.title: Walker-Leawere to start as Hurricanes name side for Super Rugby Pacific final
item.description: Walker-Leawere to start as Hurricanes name side for Super Rugby Pacific final

item.title: Messi scores first World Cup hat-trick as Argentina beats Algeria 3-0
item.description: Lionel Messi has scored his first-ever FIFA World Cup hat-trick as Argentina defeated Algeria 3-0 this afternoon. The captain once again showed his class on the world stage, scoring all three goals to guide Argentina to a convincing victory in their Group J clash.
item.content:encoded:
Lionel Messi has scored his first-ever FIFA World Cup hat-trick as Argentina defeated Algeria 3-0 this afternoon.

The captain once again showed his class on the world stage, scoring all three goals to guide Argentina to a convincing victory in their Group J clash.

The match was also a historic one for Messi, with his opening goal marking the 200th goal milestone of his remarkable career.

Playing in his sixth FIFA World Cup, Messi continues to add to his legendary status, becoming the difference-maker for Argentina as they secured all three points.

Argentina's win puts them in a strong position early in the tournament, while Algeria will look to bounce back in their next match.

Messi's hat-trick is likely to be remembered as one of the standout performances of the 2026 FIFA World Cup so far.

item.title: Delta Tigers eye strong Fiji FACT start after addressing key mistakes
item.description: Rewa FC is working to address the mistakes that have cost them crucial results as they prepare for their opening BiC Fiji FACT match, with the side determined to start the tournament strongly. Coach Priyant Manu says the side has identified lapses in concentration during key moments and poor finishing as areas that need improvement.
item.content:encoded:
Rewa FC is working to address the mistakes that have cost them crucial results as they prepare for their opening BiC Fiji FACT match, with the side determined to start the tournament strongly.

Coach Priyant Manu says the side has identified lapses in concentration during key moments and poor finishing as areas that need improvement.

He says while the Delta Tigers have performed well in matches, those costly errors have prevented them from converting strong performances into positive results.

Manu says training since last week has focused on improving the team's composure and transition play ahead of the prestigious tournament.

He adds that the return of the players who played in the OFC Pro League will also strengthen the team.

Rewa FC has won the Fiji FACT title three times, defeating Labasa 1-0 in 2011, Nadi 1-0 in 2017 and Labasa 5-3 on penalties in 2018.

They finished runners-up to Navua in 2003, Labasa in 2015, Lautoka in 2023 and Labasa again in 2025.

Meanwhile, Rewa will play their first match against Navua at 5pm this Friday, they then take on Suva at 2pm on Saturday before facing Labasa at 3pm on Sunday.

The pool matches will be played from Friday to Sunday before the competition returns to Subrail Park next weekend for the semi-finals and final.

item.title: Austria end 36-year wait for World Cup win
item.description: Austria have won a match at the FIFA World Cup finals for the first time since 1990 as they beat debutants Jordan 3-1 in San Franc

item.title: Haaland scores twice as Norway beat Iraq 4-1
item.description: Star striker Erling Haaland scored twice in his FIFA World Cup debut as Norway beat Iraq 4-1 lead in their Group I clash.Haaland o

item.title: Messi gives Argentina 1-0 halftime lead over Algeria
item.description: Lionel Messi's first-half strike has given Argentina a 1-0 lead over Algeria at halftime in their opening FIFA World Cup Group J clash. The Argentine captain opened the scoring in the 17th minute, calmly finding the back of the net to put the South Americans ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Lionel Messi's first-half strike has given Argentina a 1-0 lead over Algeria at halftime in their opening FIFA World Cup Group J clash.

The Argentine captain opened the scoring in the 17th minute, calmly finding the back of the net to put the South Americans ahead.

Argentina dominated much of the first half with Messi pulling the strings in midfield and creating several attacking opportunities as Algeria struggled to break through a disciplined Argentine defence.

Algeria attempted to respond with a number of attacking moves but were unable to find an equaliser before the break.

The Group J encounter remains finely poised heading into the second half, with Argentina looking to secure all three points while Algeria searches for a way back into the contest.

Meanwhile, France earlier defeated Senegal 3-1, while Norway beat Iraq 4-1.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Fijian Ferrari Jiuta Wainiqolo blazes to Top 14 Golden Boot with 18 tries
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijian winger and current Lyon star, Jiuta Wainiqolo commonly known as the Fijian Ferrari, ended his 2025/26 Top 14 season in France in outstanding fashion, finishing as the competition’s leading try scorer with 18 tries. The former Ratu Kadavulevu School student, who helped his school win the Dean’s Trophy in 2015, scored one try in every game for the first 7 games.
item.content:encoded:
FIJI Water Flying Fijian winger and current Lyon star, Jiuta Wainiqolo commonly known as the Fijian Ferrari, ended his 2025/26 Top 14 season in France in outstanding fashion, finishing as the competition’s leading try scorer with 18 tries.

The former Ratu Kadavulevu School student, who helped his school win the Dean’s Trophy in 2015, scored one try in every game for the first 7 games.

He showcased his blistering pace, attacking flair, and finishing ability to become one of the league’s most dangerous players.

Wainiqolo went on to score 18 tries in 18 regular-season appearances during the 2025/26 French Top 14 campaign, averaging an impressive one try per match.

He also etched his name into the record books by setting a new Top 14 record for the fastest hat-trick, scoring three tries in just 7 minutes and 23 seconds against Montauban.
